---
title: "SO_R003: Application Structure Standard Rule (L1 Basic)"
subtitle: "L1 Basic tier applications must follow a standardized directory structure, specifically must include scripts/global_scripts/ as the connection point for shared code libraries"
chapter: "CH01"
category: "rule"
number: "SO_R003"
date-created: "2025-08-18"
date-modified: "2025-08-18"
author: "Claude"
type: "rule"
law: "Structure & Organization Law"
article: 
  number: 3
  title: "Application Structure Standard Rule"
related_to:
  - "SO_P001: Layered Architecture Principle"
  - "SO_R001: Directory Structure Rule"
  - "SO_R002: Git Management Strategy"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article 3: Application Structure Standard Rule (L1 Basic) {#overview}

**Structure & Organization Law Article 3 (Application Structure Standard Rule)**

L1 Basic tier applications must follow a standardized directory structure, specifically must include `scripts/global_scripts/` as the connection point for shared code libraries.

:::{.callout-important}
## Rule Overview
L1 Basic tier applications must follow a standardized directory structure, specifically must include `scripts/global_scripts/` as the connection point for shared code libraries.
:::

# Section 1: Scope {#scope}

- **Applicable to**: All applications under the l1_basic directory
- **Not applicable to**: l2_pro and l4_enterprise (these tiers may have different architectural requirements)

# Section 2: Mandatory Structure Requirements {#mandatory-structure}

Every L1 Basic tier application must include:

```
app_name/
├── scripts/
│   └── global_scripts/     # [Mandatory] global_scripts repository
├── app.R                   # [Mandatory] Main application
├── README.md               # [Mandatory] Documentation
└── .gitignore              # [Mandatory] Git ignore rules
```

# Section 3: Global Scripts Integration Methods {#global-scripts-integration}

## Subsection 1: Git Submodule (Recommended) {#git-submodule}

```bash
cd app_name
mkdir -p scripts
cd scripts
git submodule add git@github.com:kiki830621/precision_marketing_global_scripts.git global_scripts
```

## Subsection 2: Git Subrepo {#git-subrepo}

```bash
cd app_name
git subrepo clone git@github.com:kiki830621/precision_marketing_global_scripts.git scripts/global_scripts
```

## Subsection 3: Direct Copy (Development Stage Only) {#direct-copy}

```bash
cd app_name
mkdir -p scripts
cp -r ../global_scripts scripts/
```

# Section 4: Code Reference Standards {#code-reference-standard}

## Subsection 1: Correct Practice {#correct-practice}

```r
# Correct: Use path relative to app.R
source("scripts/global_scripts/22_initializations/sc_initialization_app_mode.R")
```

## Subsection 2: Incorrect Practice {#incorrect-practice}

```r
# Incorrect: Use absolute path
source("/Users/xxx/global_scripts/...")

# Incorrect: Assume global_scripts is in parent directory
source("../global_scripts/...")
```

# Section 5: Violation Consequences {#violation-consequences}

1. Application cannot be deployed independently
2. Code sharing mechanism fails
3. Version control becomes chaotic
4. Team collaboration becomes difficult

# Section 6: Exemption Conditions {#exemption-conditions}

None. This is a mandatory rule, all applications must comply.

# Section 7: Validation Method {#validation-method}

```bash
# Automatic check script
if [ ! -d "scripts/global_scripts" ]; then
  echo "ERROR: Missing required scripts/global_scripts directory"
  exit 1
fi
```

# Section 8: Related Principles {#related-principles}

- **SO_P001: Layered Architecture Principle** - L1 Basic is part of the layered architecture
- **SO_R001: Directory Structure Rule** - Defines overall directory structure standards
- **SO_R002: Git Management Strategy** - Defines Git management methods

---

*Based on: Structure & Organization Law Article 3*  
*Related to: [SO_P001 Layered Architecture Principle](../principles/SO_P001_layered_architecture.qmd), [SO_R001 Directory Structure Rule](SO_R001_directory_structure.qmd)*
